records.title: Current research profile
records.text: Haoqi He works on LLM post-training, agentic reinforcement learning, verifier-aware harnesses, agent security, model-native safety, Agent for Security, and trustworthy AI. This is the owner-confirmed current positioning for the homepage.

records.title: Current experience and education
records.text: Haoqi He joined Sangfor Innovation Research Institute in July 2026. He worked on AI safety at ByteDance from September 2025 through June 2026. He completed a Bachelor of Engineering in Cybersecurity at Sun Yat-sen University in June 2026.

records.title: FrontierRiskWorld claim boundary
records.text: FrontierRiskWorld is an ongoing verifier-aware research system for selective risk scoring, evidence guards, routing, abstention, and fallback to real execution. Prototype or partial evaluation evidence must not be generalized into a broad claim that the system solves long-horizon agent safety. Parse success alone is not semantic correctness.

records.title: REPCORE public boundary
records.text: REPCORE is an ongoing project in Haoqi He's post-training and agent-safety research portfolio. There is not yet a citable public artifact in this repository, so the homepage does not state detailed mechanisms, numerical results, or venue claims.

records.title: Q-Detection: IJCAI 2025 official proceedings
records.text: Q-Detection: A Quantum-Classical Hybrid Poisoning Attack Detection Method is a Main Track paper at IJCAI 2025 by Haoqi He, Xiaokai Lin, Jiancai Chen, and Yan Xiao, pages 5327–5335. It presents a quantum-classical poisoning defense and evaluates label manipulation and backdoor attacks. Its more-than-20-percent speedup statement is a theoretical expectation using quantum computing power, not a measured production speedup.

records.title: ECCC: ACL Anthology record
records.text: ECCC: Edge Code Cloak Coder for Privacy Code Agent is a WiNLP 2025 paper by Haoqi He, Wenzhi Xu, Ruoying Liu, Jiarui Tang, Bairu Li, and Xiaokai Lin, pages 65–74. It combines a lightweight local model, AST-based anonymization, edge-side privacy validation, and cloud delegation of abstracted tasks. The paper reports correctness within 4–5 percentage points of evaluated GPT-4-based frameworks and a 10/10 privacy score under its stated evaluation.

records.title: HiQ-Lip: AAAI 2026 official proceedings
records.text: HiQ-Lip: A Hierarchical Quantum-Classical Method for Global Lipschitz Constant Estimation of ReLU Networks is an AAAI 2026 paper by Haoqi He, Yan Xiao, Wenzhi Xu, Ruoying Liu, Xiaokai Lin, and Kai Wen. It appears in Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence, volume 40, issue 26, pages 21628–21636, DOI 10.1609/aaai.v40i26.39312. The method converts estimation to QUBO and uses multilevel graph coarsening and refinement; result claims are bounded to the networks and solver settings reported in the paper.

records.title: Probabilistic Quantum SVM arXiv record
records.text: Probabilistic Quantum SVM Training on Ising Machine is arXiv:2503.16363 by Haoqi He and Yan Xiao. It proposes an energy-based probabilistic QUBO approach with batching and multi-batch ensembling for limited qubit counts. Numerical statements must be attributed to the simulations, real-machine experiments, datasets, and baselines described in the preprint.

records.title: QCU-Net Springer record
records.text: Quantum Lung Segmentation: QCU-Net Applied to Chest X-Ray Images is an AAIM 2024 chapter by Haoqi He and Mingkai Huang, pages 197–208 in the Springer proceedings.

records.title: Quantum Annealing and GNN for TSP Springer record
records.text: Quantum Annealing and GNN for Solving TSP with QUBO is an AAIM 2024 chapter by Haoqi He, pages 134–145 in the Springer proceedings.

records.title: Q-Detection public code repository
records.text: The public Q-Detection repository contains implementations and benchmark entry points for Q-Detection, DCM, LossScan, AutoencoderScan, and Meta-Sift under label-flipping, Narcissus, and BadNets scenarios. Repository documentation is implementation evidence and does not supersede the peer-reviewed paper for scientific claims.

candidates.name: Alignment/Lipschitz
candidates.reason: No verified public artifact supplied beyond HiQ-Lip.

candidates.name: Agent Security/RL
candidates.reason: Kept as a research direction, not a standalone result claim.
